cluster_context:
PDF annotation and markup tools in pdfFiller's browser-based editor, activated via the Edit Mode toggle. Tools split across two access points:

Tools menu: Highlight, Draw, Line, Arrow, Sticky Note, Blackout, Watermark, Date Stamp, Page Numbering, Bates Numbering
Main toolbar: Circle, Checkmark, Cross, Text Box, Erase

Functional groupings:
Markup: Highlight, Draw, Line, Arrow — visual emphasis layers
Symbols: Checkmark, Cross, Circle — one-click placement for completing non-interactive/scanned forms
Text: Text Box — static free-floating text annotation (not a fillable field)
Redaction: Blackout — permanently hides content on finalization
Document-wide: Watermark, Date Stamp, Page Numbering, Bates Numbering — apply across all pages
Remove: Erase — removes placed annotations only, not original PDF content

All tools overlay the document visually; underlying content is never altered. All tools work on both native and scanned PDFs regardless of whether text is selectable.

Blackout exception: hidden content is unrecoverable after download/finalization. Undo is available only within the active editing session.

Content accuracy rules:
- Text Box is a static annotation, not a fillable field — fillable fields are created in Add Fields mode
- Erase removes annotations only, not original document content
- Sticky Note does not print by default
- Watermark/Date Stamp/Bates are document-wide operations — treat distinctly from per-element tools
- Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, Smallpdf, PDF Expert, Foxit — differentiate on pdfFiller's browser-based, no-install access combined with annotation + signing + sharing in one workflow

cluster_context:
PDF file size optimization tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessible via two paths:
Editor header: Compress button in the top toolbar (requires active editing session)
All Tools: accessible from the My Documents dashboard (no open document required)

Mechanism: optimizes embedded images, flattens annotation and form field layers, applies file-level compression. Page content, text, structure, annotations, and form field data remain intact.

Quality levels (plan-dependent):
High — minimal visual change, modest size reduction
Medium — balanced size/quality tradeoff
Low — maximum size reduction, most visible image quality impact

Yield varies by content type: image-heavy and scanned PDFs compress significantly; native text-only PDFs yield minimal reduction; already-optimized files yield near-zero reduction.

Post-compression: user can download immediately or continue editing in the same session.

Content accuracy rules:
- Never promise specific size reduction percentages — yield depends on source document image density and optimization state
- Quality loss applies to image fidelity only, not text, structure, or form field data
- Compress, Delete Pages, Split, and Save As each solve a different problem — do not treat them as interchangeable
- Compress preserves PDF format — format conversion is Save As's function
- Do not imply compression is unconditionally free — quality level selection is plan-tier dependent
- Named competitors: Smallpdf, ilovepdf, Adobe Acrobat (Standard/Pro), Foxit — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one compress + edit + sign workflow

cluster_context:
PDF format conversion tool in pdfFiller. Accessible via three paths:
Done dropdown (editor): Done → Save As → choose output format
All Tools (dashboard): Convert to Word / Excel / PowerPoint / Images card (no open document required)
More menu (document row): document-level conversion without entering the editor

Supported conversions:
PDF → Word (.docx), Excel (.xlsx), PowerPoint (.pptx), Image (.jpg / .png)
Non-PDF → PDF: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and image files convert to PDF automatically on upload

Mechanism: server-side conversion, downloads immediately after processing. No software installation required.

Content accuracy rules:
- Conversion quality depends on source document type: native (digitally created) PDFs convert with higher text and layout fidelity; scanned PDFs produce image-based output in Word unless OCR is applied — do not promise editable text from scanned sources
- Layout fidelity is not guaranteed: complex formatting (columns, tables, embedded graphics) may reflow in Word or Excel output
- Image conversion produces one .jpg/.png file per page, not a single image for multi-page documents
- Do not imply all conversions are unconditionally free — plan-tier restrictions may apply
- Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Nitro, Foxit — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one convert + edit + sign workflow, not on conversion speed or OCR accuracy claims

cluster_context:
Document template creation in pdfFiller — building a reusable fillable document structure for a specific document type and saving it for repeated use.

Two creation paths:
Upload new file: upload a document from device or cloud → open in editor → use Add Fields mode to add fillable fields → save as template
From existing document: My Docs → More menu → Tools → Convert to Template → choose Use As Is or Add Fields → save to Templates

Templates are saved to Personal Templates (accessible to the creator only) or Team Templates (shared across all workspace members). The original template is preserved on each reuse — every completion saves as a new document, not over the template.

Reuse workflow: open template → parties fill their fields → document is completed, signed, and saved or sent. Templates can also be distributed as a fill link via LinkToFill.

Add Fields mode is the core of template creation: text fields, checkboxes, dropdown menus, signature fields, date fields, initials, and required-field designation.

Content accuracy rules:
- A template in pdfFiller is a fillable document structure — "create" means building the fillable form layer, not authoring legally reviewed document text
- Do not imply pdfFiller provides jurisdiction-specific legal content — the template structure is the product; document text comes from the user's uploaded file or their own input
- Personal vs. Team templates have different access scope — do not conflate them
- LinkToFill distributes a template as a public or restricted fill link — distinct from sending a PDF attachment
- Named competitors: DocuSign, JotForm, PandaDoc, Adobe Acrobat Sign — differentiate on pdfFiller's browser-based template builder that combines document editing + fillable fields + reuse + e-sign in one workflow

cluster_context:
PDF text editing tools in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via the Edit button (pencil icon) in the editor toolbar, which activates Edit Mode. Covers direct modification of document content — editing, adding, and removing text — and text formatting. Distinct from group_annotate, which overlays content without altering source text; and from group_fillable_fields, which creates interactive form elements.
Core capabilities:
Edit existing text — click directly on text blocks in native PDFs to modify characters, words, or entire blocks
Add text — insert new free-floating text blocks anywhere on the document
Delete/remove text — select and delete existing text content from the document
Whiteout — covers underlying content with a white fill rectangle for correction purposes
Text formatting: font family, size, bold, italic, underline, text color, text alignment
Find & Replace — locate and replace specific text strings across the document
Native vs. scanned PDFs:
Native (digitally created) PDFs: full text editing with best-effort font matching
Scanned PDFs: text is image-based — direct editing unavailable without OCR; annotation tools should be used to overlay corrections
Content accuracy rules:

Edit Mode modifies document content directly; annotation tools overlay without modifying — never conflate the two
Text editing on scanned PDFs requires OCR (group_scan_ocr) — do not promise direct text editing on scanned sources
Whiteout ≠ Blackout — Whiteout is a correction tool; Blackout (group_annotate) is permanent redaction
Font matching is best-effort — exact font reproduction is not guaranteed
Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, Foxit, Nitro, Sejda — differentiate on pdfFiller's browser-based, no-install access

cluster_context:
Image manipulation tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via the Image icon in the main toolbar — clicking it opens a file selection window; the selected image is placed on the active page as a movable, resizable overlay element.

Supported input formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF (BMP in some environments).

Operations available after placement:
- Resize: drag corner handles; aspect ratio preserved by default
- Reposition: drag image to any location on the page
- Rotate: freely rotate the image element
- Layer order: bring forward / send backward relative to other page elements
- Opacity: adjust image transparency (plan-dependent)
- Duplicate / Delete: copy or remove the image element

Crop and cut-out operations have no dedicated dialog. They are performed by resizing the image element to expose the desired area, or by deleting the element and re-uploading a pre-cropped image. "Merge photo" and "edit photo" keyword variants map to the same Image Tool workflow: upload → place → resize/reposition. There is no standalone merge or photo editor.

Content accuracy rules:
- Do not describe a separate crop, cut-out, or replace image tool — these operations use Image Tool's resize and re-upload workflow
- Opacity control is plan-dependent — do not imply it is universally available
- Image Tool adds images as overlay elements; it does not embed them into the underlying PDF content layer
- Supported formats are JPEG, PNG, GIF — do not claim support for SVG, TIFF, or WebP
- Named competitors in this keyword space: Adobe Acrobat, PDF24, ilovepdf, Smallpdf — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one edit + sign + share workflow, not on image editing depth

cluster_context:
PDF merging tool in pdfFiller's browser-based platform. Combines multiple files into a single PDF without installing software.

Access paths:
- Dashboard: Merge quick-action card (no document open required)
- Document context: Tools → Merge (from the More menu while viewing a document in MyDocs)
- Dashboard: All Tools → Merge

Supported input formats: PDF, Word (.doc/.docx), Excel (.xlsx), and image files — non-PDF inputs are automatically converted to PDF during the merge process.

Workflow: Add files from local device or MyDocs → review and reorder pages using drag-and-drop thumbnails → click Merge (dashboard flow) or Merge Now (document context flow) → download immediately or save to MyDocs.

Key capabilities:
- Select files from local device and MyDocs in the same session
- Reorder pages across all added files before generating the final document
- Preview page thumbnails before committing to the merge
- Output is always a single, consolidated PDF

Content accuracy rules:
- Input format conversion (Word, Excel, images → PDF) is automatic but fidelity depends on source document complexity — do not promise pixel-perfect or layout-perfect conversion
- Page reordering uses file-level thumbnails in the Merge interface, before output is generated — do not describe this as the Rearrange Pages tool or as in-editor page management
- The Merge tool generates a new combined PDF; source documents in MyDocs remain unchanged
- Merge and Rearrange Pages are distinct tools: Merge combines separate files; Rearrange reorganizes pages within a single existing document — do not conflate them
- Do not imply merging is unconditionally free — plan tier may affect file size limits or available features
- Named competitors: ilovepdf, Adobe Acrobat Online, Smallpdf, PDF24 — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one merge + edit + sign + share workflow, not on merge speed or file count claims

cluster_context:
PDF creation capabilities in pdfFiller's browser-based platform. "Creating a PDF" covers multiple entry paths — there is no single monolithic creation tool; the path depends on the user's starting point.

Creation paths:
- Start blank: Add New → Blank document → opens an empty PDF in the full editor (add text, images, annotations, form fields)
- Upload & convert: Add New → Upload document (or drag & drop on dashboard) → supports Word (.doc/.docx), Excel (.xlsx), PowerPoint (.pptx), and images; non-PDF files are automatically converted to PDF on upload
- Cloud import: Add New → Import from cloud → connects to Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, or Box; imported non-PDF files are converted automatically
- From template: Add New → Create from template → opens a pre-built form or document for editing

After any creation path, the document opens in the full editor for text placement, image insertion, annotation, and form field creation.

Creating a fillable PDF: after opening any document, switch to Add Fields mode → drag and drop signature, text, date, dropdown, checkbox, and other field types onto the page → save or convert to a reusable template.

Content accuracy rules:
- pdfFiller is a browser-based PDF creator — it does not install a virtual printer driver on the user's system; do not describe it as a PDF printer or desktop application
- The "do pdf" keyword variant targets users familiar with doPDF desktop software — differentiate on browser access, no installation, and the all-in-one edit + sign + share workflow
- Upload format conversion (Word, Excel, images → PDF) is automatic but layout fidelity depends on source document complexity — do not promise pixel-perfect conversion
- Creating a fillable PDF requires Add Fields mode — it is a distinct step from simply opening or uploading a document; do not imply any document automatically becomes fillable
- Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, Smallpdf, PDF24, ilovepdf, doPDF — differentiate on browser-based access with no installation and the complete create + edit + fill + sign workflow in one platform

cluster_context:
The group_protect cluster covers pdfFiller's PDF password security features. The primary tool is Protect Document (Tools → Protect Document): users select a document and page scope — entire document or specific pages — enter a password, and click Apply. The protected PDF then requires that password to open, whether downloaded locally or shared via a pdfFiller link. Product documentation also describes the feature as "add restrictions to prevent edits," making it the platform's mechanism for creating read-only access for external recipients.

A secondary security feature is the Confidential folder in My Documents. It requires phone verification and a custom password to access, and hides documents from Recent and main document lists. This is workspace-level access control inside pdfFiller — not a PDF file-level password.

Accuracy rules: 1. Protect Document adds an open-entry password to the PDF file; it does not prevent printing or screenshots by authorized openers. 2. The Confidential folder is workspace security (in-app), not file-level PDF encryption. Do not conflate the two. 3. "Unlock PDF" in pdfFiller means opening a file the user already knows the password for — the platform is not a password cracker or bypass tool. 4. "Make PDF read-only" is accomplished via Protect Document — recipients without the password cannot open or modify the file. 5. Access path is Tools → Protect Document, not a right-click menu or the Edit toolbar. 6. Top SERP competitors are ilovepdf (#1), Smallpdf (#2), pdf2go (#4), and Adobe (#7) — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one workflow: protect, fill, sign, and share without switching tools.

cluster_context:
Page management tools in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Covers all structural page operations — reordering, rotating, deleting, inserting, and duplicating — without creating separate files.

Access paths:
- Dashboard: Rearrange quick-action card (no document open required)
- Document context: Tools → Rearrange pages (More menu in MyDocs)
- Inside editor: Manage button (under page thumbnails) → Manage modal for batch/precise operations
- Inside editor: Sidebar thumbnail controls for quick single-page actions

Operations available via the Manage modal and/or sidebar:
- Reorder: drag-and-drop grid, Move left/right, Move to (jump to specific page number), multi-select; sidebar Move Up/Down arrows for quick single shifts
- Rotate: 90° clockwise per click (achieves 90°/180°/270°); single or multiple pages; preview before Apply
- Delete: sidebar trash icon (single page, instant); Manage modal batch delete; confirmation dialog triggers if page contains form fields
- Add blank page: sidebar "+" buttons between thumbnails (precise placement); "Add blank page" button at sidebar bottom (appends to end); Manage modal "Add page" (inserts after selected page); result is a fully editable blank page
- Duplicate: copies all page content including text, images, annotations, and form fields; copies appear at end and can be reordered

All Manage modal changes are previewed before committing — Apply saves, Cancel discards all changes.

Content accuracy rules:
- All operations modify the existing document in place — they do not create separate files (that is Split's function)
- Rotate increments at 90° clockwise per click only — do not imply free-angle or arbitrary rotation
- Deleting a page with form fields triggers a confirmation dialog — do not imply deletion is always instant or field-safe without warning
- Add Blank Page inserts an empty editable page — do not imply it imports or appends pages from another document (that is Merge's function)
- Duplicate copies full page content including form fields — do not imply it produces a clean blank copy
- Named competitors: ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Adobe Acrobat, PDF24, Sejda — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one page management + edit + sign + share workflow in one platform

cluster_context:
E-signature request workflow in pdfFiller. Sends a document to one or multiple named recipients for legally binding electronic signing via a secure email link — recipients do not need a pdfFiller account.

Access paths:
- Dashboard: "Request e-signatures" quick-action card (no document open required)
- MyDocs: document action menu → Request e-signatures
- Inside editor: Done dropdown → E-sign
- Inside editor (Add fields mode): Request E-Signatures panel → Review and Send

Workflow: Open document → switch to Add fields mode → add recipients by email and assign roles (Signer or Viewer) → assign signature, initials, date, and form fields to each recipient → configure signing order (sequential or parallel) → set reminders and expiration deadline (optional) → click Review and Send → each signer receives a secure email link → sender tracks progress from Sent → E-signature requests.

Key capabilities: multi-signer support with per-recipient field assignment (each signer sees only their assigned fields); sequential or parallel signing order; automated reminders and expiration deadlines; real-time signing status tracking; audit trail stored with the signed document; signed PDF auto-saved to sender's MyDocs.

Content accuracy rules:
- Recipients do not need a pdfFiller account to sign — access is via a one-time secure email link
- "Send to sign" and "Request e-signatures" refer to the same workflow — do not treat them as separate features
- Signing order is configurable (sequential or parallel) — do not imply only one mode is available
- Audit trail availability may be plan-dependent — do not unconditionally promise it across all tiers
- Do not conflate Request e-signatures (routing to others) with the Sign tool (signing yourself) — these are distinct workflows
- Do not conflate with LinkToFill — LinkToFill enables mass anonymous form collection; Request e-signatures is for named-recipient, individually routed signing
- Named competitors: DocuSign, Adobe Acrobat Sign, PandaDoc, SignWell, Dropbox Sign — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one edit + fill + request + track workflow without switching platforms

cluster_context:
Electronic signature tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via the Signature button in the main editor toolbar (Edit Mode). Covers the self-sign flow — adding the user's own signature or initials — not requesting others' signatures (that is Send to Sign).

Three signing methods:
Draw — freehand using mouse, touchpad, or stylus
Type — styled text with selectable fonts and colors
Upload — image file of an existing handwritten signature

Initials: separate option with the same three input methods.

Customization:
Draw: color and line thickness
Type: font style and color
All: resize and reposition after placement
Save & Reuse: signatures saved to account for reuse; user can set a default signature.
Placement: drag to position, resize via corner handles, removable like other placed elements.
Legal validity: compliant with ESIGN, UETA, eIDAS, and GDPR. Signed documents receive a timestamped audit trail and Verified by pdfFiller badge.
Content accuracy rules:
- Self-sign flow ≠ Send to Sign — do not describe recipient requesting here
- All three methods (Draw/Type/Upload) are equally legally binding — do not rank one as more official
- Saved signature ≠ auto-applied — user places it manually per document
- Signature tool ≠ Signature Field — fields for others to fill are created in Add Fields mode
- Audit trail generated at download/finalization
- Named competitors: DocuSign, Adobe Acrobat Sign, Dropbox Sign, signNow — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one sign + fill + edit workflow no separate app required

cluster_context:
PDF splitting and page extraction tool in pdfFiller's browser-based platform. Separates a multi-page PDF into smaller files without modifying the source document.

Access paths:
- Dashboard: Split quick-action card (no document open required)
- Document context: Tools → Split (from the More menu in MyDocs)

Three split modes:
- Extract pages: select specific individual pages to save as a new standalone PDF
- Split by range: define custom page ranges (e.g., pages 1–3, 4–7, 8–end), each range becoming a separate named output file
- Split by all pages: every page of the document becomes its own individual PDF

Additional capabilities: rename each output file before generating; output files are saved to MyDocs; when many pages are split individually, output files are packaged as a ZIP archive for download.

The source document remains unchanged in MyDocs after any split operation — new files are created separately.

Content accuracy rules:
- "Extract pages" and "Split by range" are distinct modes within the same Split tool — do not describe them as separate tools
- The source PDF is not modified or deleted after splitting — a new file is created for each output
- When many pages are split individually, output is delivered as a ZIP package — do not describe each file as a separate standalone download in this case
- Split and Delete Pages are distinct: Split creates new separate files from a source; Delete Pages removes pages from within the existing document — do not conflate them
- Split and Rearrange Pages are distinct: Split separates a document into multiple files; Rearrange reorders pages within a single existing document
- Named competitors: ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Adobe Acrobat Online, PDF24, Sejda — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one split + edit + sign + share workflow without switching platforms

cluster_context:
Watermark tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via Tools → Watermark in the editor toolbar.

Two watermark types:
- Text watermark: enter any text (e.g., "CONFIDENTIAL", "DRAFT", "COPY", company name)
- Image watermark: upload a logo or emblem as an image overlay

Customization options: size (scaling), color, transparency/opacity, rotation (e.g., 45° diagonal or horizontal), page scope (current page, all pages, or a custom page range).

Edit or remove: reopen Tools → Watermark to modify or delete a previously applied watermark.

The Watermark Panel also contains three document-wide stamping tools:
- Date Stamp: inserts today's date or a custom date on every page; configurable format (MM/DD/YYYY, etc.), placement (header/footer, left/center/right), font, and transparency
- Page Numbering: adds sequential page numbers to all pages in formats like "1", "Page 1 of 6"; placement and font configurable
- Bates Numbering: adds uniquely incremented identifiers with custom prefix, suffix, digit padding, and start number — used for legal discovery, archival, and evidence set management

Removing watermarks from existing documents: watermarks applied via pdfFiller's tool can be deleted by reopening Tools → Watermark. Visible watermark text embedded in an uploaded document can be covered using the Erase tool.

Content accuracy rules:
- The Watermark tool adds a semi-transparent overlay — do not claim pdfFiller watermarks are non-removable, security-grade, or DRM-enforced
- pdfFiller cannot remove cryptographically embedded or DRM-protected watermarks — the Erase tool covers visible watermark text visually only; it does not extract or strip embedded data
- Date Stamp, Page Numbering, and Bates Numbering are accessed through the Watermark Panel — clarify this connection when these features appear alongside watermark keywords
- Bates Numbering is a legal/archival-specific feature — do not describe it as a general page stamp or watermark
- Named competitors: ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Sejda, Adobe Acrobat, PDF24 — differentiate on pdfFiller's all-in-one watermark + edit + sign + share workflow in a single platform
